framework.name: NIST CSF
framework.description: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 — comprehensive cybersecurity risk management

controls.title: Organizational Mission Understanding
controls.requirementText: The organizational mission is understood and informs cybersecurity risk management
controls.guidance: Ensure that the organization's mission, objectives, and activities are documented and that cybersecurity risk management decisions are aligned with and support those objectives.
evidenceRequirements: Risk management policy aligned to mission

controls.title: Stakeholder Expectations
controls.requirementText: Internal and external stakeholders are understood, and their needs and expectations regarding cybersecurity risk management are understood and considered
controls.guidance: Identify and document all internal and external stakeholders whose expectations influence cybersecurity risk decisions, including regulators, customers, partners, and employees.

controls.title: Legal and Regulatory Requirements
controls.requirementText: Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding cybersecurity — including privacy and civil liberties obligations — are understood and managed
controls.guidance: Maintain a current inventory of applicable legal, regulatory, and contractual cybersecurity obligations and ensure compliance is monitored and managed.

controls.title: Critical Objectives and Services
controls.requirementText: Critical objectives, capabilities, and services that stakeholders depend on or expect from the organization are understood and communicated
controls.guidance: Identify and document the organization's critical business services and capabilities, and communicate dependencies to relevant stakeholders.

controls.title: Dependency Understanding
controls.requirementText: Outcomes, capabilities, and services that the organization depends on are understood and communicated
controls.guidance: Identify external dependencies including third-party services, supply chain elements, and infrastructure that the organization relies upon for its operations.

controls.title: Risk Management Objectives
controls.requirementText: Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
controls.guidance: Define and document risk management objectives that are approved by senior leadership and aligned with the organization's overall mission and strategy.

controls.title: Risk Appetite and Tolerance
controls.requirementText: Risk appetite and risk tolerance statements are established, communicated, and maintained
controls.guidance: Define quantitative and qualitative risk appetite and tolerance levels, communicate them across the organization, and review them periodically.

controls.title: Enterprise Risk Management Integration
controls.requirementText: Cybersecurity risk management activities and outcomes are included in enterprise risk management processes
controls.guidance: Integrate cybersecurity risk management into the organization's overall enterprise risk management program to ensure holistic risk visibility.

controls.title: Strategic Risk Response Direction
controls.requirementText: Strategic direction that describes appropriate risk response options is established and communicated
controls.guidance: Establish and communicate strategic guidance on acceptable risk response options including accept, mitigate, transfer, and avoid.

controls.title: Risk Communication Lines
controls.requirementText: Lines of communication across the organization are established for cybersecurity risks, including risks from suppliers and other third parties
controls.guidance: Establish formal communication channels for reporting and escalating cybersecurity risks across all levels of the organization and with external parties.

controls.title: Standardized Risk Calculation
controls.requirementText: A standardized method for calculating, documenting, categorizing, and prioritizing cybersecurity risks is established and communicated
controls.guidance: Implement a consistent methodology for risk assessment including standardized scoring, categorization, and prioritization criteria.

controls.title: Strategic Opportunities
controls.requirementText: Strategic opportunities (i.e., positive risks) are characterized and are included in organizational cybersecurity risk discussions
controls.guidance: Include positive risk opportunities in cybersecurity risk assessments to identify potential strategic advantages from cybersecurity investments.

controls.title: Leadership Accountability
controls.requirementText: Organizational leadership is responsible and accountable for cybersecurity risk and fosters a culture that is risk-aware, ethical, and continually improving
controls.guidance: Ensure executive leadership has defined accountability for cybersecurity risk and actively promotes a security-aware culture throughout the organization.

controls.title: Cybersecurity Roles and Responsibilities
controls.requirementText: Roles, responsibilities, and authorities related to cybersecurity risk management are established, communicated, understood, and enforced
controls.guidance: Define, document, and communicate cybersecurity roles and responsibilities across all organizational levels including RACI matrices and authority designations.

controls.title: Adequate Resource Allocation
controls.requirementText: Adequate resources are allocated commensurate with the cybersecurity risk strategy, roles, responsibilities, and policies
controls.guidance: Ensure sufficient budget, personnel, tools, and training are allocated to support the cybersecurity program aligned with the risk strategy.

controls.title: Human Resources Practices
controls.requirementText: Cybersecurity is included in human resources practices
controls.guidance: Integrate cybersecurity considerations into HR processes including hiring, onboarding, role changes, and termination procedures.

controls.title: Cybersecurity Policy Establishment
controls.requirementText: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is established based on organizational context, cybersecurity strategy, and priorities and is communicated and enforced
controls.guidance: Develop comprehensive cybersecurity policies that reflect organizational context, strategy, and risk priorities, and ensure they are communicated to and enforced across all relevant personnel.

controls.title: Policy Review and Update
controls.requirementText: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is reviewed, updated, communicated, and enforced to reflect changes in requirements, threats, technology, and organizational mission
controls.guidance: Establish a regular review cycle for cybersecurity policies to ensure they remain current with evolving threats, technology changes, and organizational requirements.

controls.title: Supply Chain Risk Management Program
controls.requirementText: A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program, strategy, objectives, policies, and processes are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
controls.guidance: Establish a formal C-SCRM program with documented strategy, objectives, and processes approved by organizational leadership.

controls.title: Supplier Prioritization
controls.requirementText: Suppliers are known and prioritized by criticality
controls.guidance: Maintain an inventory of all suppliers and prioritize them based on their criticality to the organization's operations and data security.

controls.title: Supply Chain Contractual Requirements
controls.requirementText: Requirements to address cybersecurity risks in supply chains are established, prioritized, and integrated into contracts and other types of agreements with suppliers and other relevant third parties
controls.guidance: Include cybersecurity requirements in contracts, SLAs, and agreements with suppliers and third parties covering security controls, incident notification, and audit rights.

controls.title: Supplier Risk Monitoring
controls.requirementText: The risks posed by a supplier, their products and services, and other third parties are understood, recorded, prioritized, assessed, responded to, and monitored over the course of the relationship
controls.guidance: Implement ongoing monitoring and assessment of supplier risks throughout the lifecycle of the relationship including periodic reassessments.

controls.title: Risk Management Strategy Review
controls.requirementText: Cybersecurity risk management strategy outcomes are reviewed to inform and adjust strategy and direction
controls.guidance: Regularly review the outcomes of cybersecurity risk management activities and use findings to adjust the overall strategy and direction.

controls.title: Strategy Coverage Review
controls.requirementText: The cybersecurity risk management strategy is reviewed and adjusted to ensure coverage of organizational requirements and risks
controls.guidance: Periodically assess whether the cybersecurity risk management strategy adequately addresses all organizational requirements and emerging risks.

controls.title: Performance Evaluation
controls.requirementText: Organizational cybersecurity risk management performance is evaluated and reviewed for adjustments needed
controls.guidance: Evaluate cybersecurity risk management performance using defined metrics and KPIs, and make adjustments as needed.

controls.title: Hardware Asset Inventory
controls.requirementText: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained
controls.guidance: Maintain accurate, up-to-date inventories of all hardware assets managed by the organization including servers, workstations, network devices, and IoT devices.

controls.title: Software Asset Inventory
controls.requirementText: Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained
controls.guidance: Maintain accurate, up-to-date inventories of all software, services, and systems including versions, licenses, and deployment locations.

controls.title: Network Communication Mapping
controls.requirementText: Representations of the organization's authorized network communication and internal and external network data flows are maintained
controls.guidance: Document and maintain up-to-date network architecture diagrams and data flow maps showing authorized communication paths.

controls.title: Asset Prioritization
controls.requirementText: Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, resources, and impact on the mission
controls.guidance: Classify and prioritize all assets based on their criticality to the organization's mission, data sensitivity, and potential impact if compromised.

controls.title: Data Inventory
controls.requirementText: Inventories of data and corresponding metadata for designated data types are maintained
controls.guidance: Maintain inventories of data assets including data classification, storage locations, retention requirements, and associated metadata.

controls.title: Asset Lifecycle Management
controls.requirementText: Systems, hardware, software, services, and data are managed throughout their life cycles
controls.guidance: Implement lifecycle management processes for all assets from procurement through decommissioning including tracking, maintenance, and secure disposal.

controls.title: Vulnerability Identification
controls.requirementText: Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded
controls.guidance: Implement processes to continuously identify, validate, and record vulnerabilities in organizational assets through scanning, testing, and threat intelligence.

controls.title: Cyber Threat Intelligence
controls.requirementText: Cyber threat intelligence is received from information sharing forums and sources
controls.guidance: Participate in threat intelligence sharing communities and subscribe to relevant threat feeds to maintain awareness of current threats and attack patterns.

controls.title: Threat Identification
controls.requirementText: Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and recorded
controls.guidance: Identify and document both internal and external threats to organizational assets, including threat actor profiles and attack vectors.

controls.title: Impact and Likelihood Assessment
controls.requirementText: Potential impacts and likelihoods of threats exploiting vulnerabilities are identified and recorded
controls.guidance: Assess and document the potential business impact and likelihood of identified threats exploiting known vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Inherent Risk Understanding
controls.requirementText: Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to understand inherent risk and inform the prioritization of risk response activities
controls.guidance: Aggregate threat, vulnerability, likelihood, and impact data to calculate inherent risk levels and prioritize risk response activities accordingly.

controls.title: Risk Response Management
controls.requirementText: Risk responses are chosen, prioritized, planned, tracked, and communicated
controls.guidance: Select appropriate risk responses (accept, mitigate, transfer, avoid), develop treatment plans, and track their implementation.

controls.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Processes
controls.requirementText: Processes for receiving, analyzing, and responding to vulnerability disclosures are established
controls.guidance: Establish formal processes for receiving and handling vulnerability disclosures from internal and external sources including responsible disclosure programs.

controls.title: Improvements from Evaluations
controls.requirementText: Improvements are identified from evaluations
controls.guidance: Use the results of cybersecurity evaluations, assessments, and audits to identify opportunities for improvement in security processes and controls.

controls.title: Improvements from Security Tests
controls.requirementText: Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and relevant third parties
controls.guidance: Conduct regular security tests, exercises, and simulations and use results to drive improvements in cybersecurity posture.

controls.title: Incident Response Plan Improvement
controls.requirementText: Incident response plans and other cybersecurity plans that affect operations are established, communicated, maintained, and improved
controls.guidance: Maintain and continuously improve incident response plans and other operational cybersecurity plans based on lessons learned and changing conditions.

controls.subdomain: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.title: Identity and Credential Management
controls.requirementText: Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed by the organization
controls.guidance: Implement identity lifecycle management for all users, services, and devices including provisioning, review, and deprovisioning of identities and credentials.

controls.subdomain: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.title: Identity Proofing
controls.requirementText: Identities are proofed and bound to credentials based on the context of interactions
controls.guidance: Verify identities before binding them to credentials using appropriate identity proofing methods based on the risk level of the interaction.

controls.subdomain: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.title: Authentication
controls.requirementText: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated
controls.guidance: Implement authentication mechanisms for all users, services, and hardware including multi-factor authentication for high-risk access.

controls.subdomain: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.title: Access Permissions Management
controls.requirementText: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined in a policy, managed, enforced, and reviewed, and incorporate the principles of least privilege and separation of duties
controls.guidance: Define and enforce access control policies based on least privilege and separation of duties, with regular reviews of access permissions and entitlements.

controls.subdomain: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.title: Physical Access Management
controls.requirementText: Physical access to assets is managed, monitored, and enforced commensurate with risk
controls.guidance: Implement physical access controls proportionate to the risk level of the assets being protected, including monitoring and logging of physical access.

controls.title: General Cybersecurity Awareness
controls.requirementText: Personnel are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform general tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind
controls.guidance: Provide regular cybersecurity awareness training to all personnel covering topics such as phishing, password hygiene, social engineering, and data handling.

controls.title: Specialized Role Training
controls.requirementText: Individuals in specialized roles are provided with awareness and training so that they possess the knowledge and skills to perform relevant tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind
controls.guidance: Provide role-specific cybersecurity training to individuals with specialized responsibilities such as system administrators, developers, and incident responders.

controls.title: Data-at-Rest Protection
controls.requirementText: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected
controls.guidance: Implement encryption, access controls, and integrity mechanisms to protect stored data across all storage media and locations.

controls.title: Data-in-Transit Protection
controls.requirementText: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected
controls.guidance: Use strong cryptographic protocols (e.g., TLS 1.2+) to protect data during transmission across all network communications.

controls.title: Data-in-Use Protection
controls.requirementText: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
controls.guidance: Implement controls to protect data while being actively processed, including memory protections, secure enclaves, and access controls on processing systems.

controls.title: Backup Management
controls.requirementText: Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested
controls.guidance: Implement comprehensive backup processes including regular backups, secure storage, integrity verification, and periodic restoration testing.

controls.title: Configuration Management
controls.requirementText: Configuration management practices are established and applied
controls.guidance: Establish and maintain secure baseline configurations for all systems and apply configuration management processes to track and control changes.

controls.title: Software Maintenance
controls.requirementText: Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk
controls.guidance: Maintain software through regular patching, replace end-of-life software, and remove unnecessary or unauthorized software from systems.

controls.title: Log Generation
controls.requirementText: Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring
controls.guidance: Configure systems to generate comprehensive log records for security-relevant events and make them available for continuous monitoring and analysis.

controls.title: Unauthorized Software Prevention
controls.requirementText: Installation and execution of unauthorized software are prevented
controls.guidance: Implement application whitelisting, software restriction policies, or other controls to prevent installation and execution of unauthorized software.

controls.title: Secure Software Development
controls.requirementText: Secure software development practices are integrated, and their performance is monitored throughout the software development life cycle
controls.guidance: Integrate secure development practices including code review, security testing, and vulnerability assessment into all phases of the SDLC.

controls.title: Network Protection
controls.requirementText: Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage
controls.guidance: Implement network segmentation, firewalls, access controls, and monitoring to protect networks from unauthorized access and usage.

controls.title: Resilience Mechanisms
controls.requirementText: Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations
controls.guidance: Implement redundancy, failover, and disaster recovery mechanisms to maintain operations during normal conditions and adverse events.

controls.title: Resource Capacity Management
controls.requirementText: Adequate resource capacity to ensure availability is maintained
controls.guidance: Monitor and manage resource capacity including processing power, storage, bandwidth, and personnel to ensure continuous availability.

controls.title: Network Monitoring
controls.requirementText: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
controls.guidance: Deploy network monitoring tools and processes to continuously monitor network traffic and services for indicators of potentially adverse events.

controls.title: Physical Environment Monitoring
controls.requirementText: The physical environment is monitored to find potentially adverse events
controls.guidance: Implement physical security monitoring including surveillance, environmental sensors, and intrusion detection for physical facilities.

controls.title: Personnel Activity Monitoring
controls.requirementText: Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events
controls.guidance: Monitor personnel activities and technology usage patterns to detect insider threats, policy violations, and other potentially adverse events.

controls.title: External Service Provider Monitoring
controls.requirementText: External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
controls.guidance: Monitor the activities and services of external service providers for security events and anomalies that could indicate adverse conditions.

controls.title: Computing Environment Monitoring
controls.requirementText: Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events
controls.guidance: Implement endpoint detection and response, file integrity monitoring, and runtime monitoring across computing environments.

controls.title: Adverse Event Analysis
controls.requirementText: Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
controls.guidance: Analyze detected adverse events to understand their nature, scope, and associated activities for effective response determination.

controls.title: Information Correlation
controls.requirementText: Information is correlated from multiple sources
controls.guidance: Implement SIEM or similar correlation capabilities to aggregate and correlate security information from multiple sources for comprehensive analysis.

controls.title: Adverse Event Information Sharing
controls.requirementText: Information on adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools
controls.guidance: Ensure that information about adverse events is promptly distributed to authorized personnel and security tools for analysis and response.

controls.title: Threat Intelligence Integration
controls.requirementText: Cyber threat intelligence and other contextual information are integrated into the analysis
controls.guidance: Integrate cyber threat intelligence feeds and contextual information into event analysis processes to enhance detection and understanding of threats.

controls.title: Incident Declaration
controls.requirementText: Incidents are declared when adverse events meet the defined incident criteria
controls.guidance: Define clear incident declaration criteria and ensure that adverse events are promptly escalated to incident status when criteria are met.

controls.title: Incident Response Plan Execution
controls.requirementText: The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared
controls.guidance: Execute the incident response plan promptly upon incident declaration, coordinating with relevant internal teams and external third parties as needed.

controls.title: Incident Triage and Validation
controls.requirementText: Incident reports are triaged and validated
controls.guidance: Establish triage processes to quickly validate and prioritize incident reports for appropriate response allocation.

controls.title: Incident Categorization
controls.requirementText: Incidents are categorized and prioritized
controls.guidance: Categorize and prioritize incidents based on severity, impact, urgency, and affected assets to guide response efforts.

controls.title: Incident Escalation
controls.requirementText: Incidents are escalated or elevated as needed
controls.guidance: Define and follow escalation procedures to ensure incidents are elevated to appropriate management levels based on severity and impact.

controls.title: Recovery Initiation Criteria
controls.requirementText: The criteria for initiating incident recovery are applied
controls.guidance: Define and apply criteria for transitioning from incident response to recovery activities based on containment and eradication status.

controls.title: Root Cause Analysis
controls.requirementText: Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause of the incident
controls.guidance: Conduct thorough analysis of incidents to determine the timeline of events, attack vectors used, and root cause of the incident.

controls.title: Investigation Record Integrity
controls.requirementText: Actions performed during an investigation are recorded, and the records' integrity and provenance are preserved
controls.guidance: Maintain detailed records of all investigation activities with integrity controls to ensure evidence admissibility and audit trail completeness.

controls.title: Incident Data Collection
controls.requirementText: Incident data and metadata are collected, and their integrity and provenance are preserved
controls.guidance: Collect and preserve incident-related data and metadata with appropriate integrity controls for analysis and potential legal proceedings.

controls.title: Stakeholder Notification
controls.requirementText: Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents
controls.guidance: Notify relevant internal and external stakeholders of incidents in accordance with notification policies, regulatory requirements, and contractual obligations.

controls.title: Information Sharing
controls.requirementText: Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders
controls.guidance: Share relevant incident information with designated stakeholders including law enforcement, regulators, and information sharing organizations as appropriate.

controls.title: Incident Containment
controls.requirementText: Incidents are contained
controls.guidance: Implement containment strategies to prevent the spread and escalation of incidents, including network isolation, account disabling, and system quarantine.

controls.title: Incident Eradication
controls.requirementText: Incidents are eradicated
controls.guidance: Remove all components of the incident including malware, compromised accounts, and unauthorized access to eliminate the threat from the environment.

controls.title: Recovery Plan Execution
controls.requirementText: The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated from the incident response process
controls.guidance: Execute the recovery plan following containment and eradication, restoring systems and services according to documented procedures and priorities.

controls.title: Recovery Action Prioritization
controls.requirementText: Recovery actions are selected, scoped, prioritized, and performed
controls.guidance: Select and prioritize recovery actions based on business impact, criticality of affected systems, and available resources.

controls.title: Backup Integrity Verification
controls.requirementText: The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before using them for restoration
controls.guidance: Verify the integrity and security of backups and restoration assets before using them to restore systems to prevent reintroduction of threats.

controls.title: Post-Incident Operational Norms
controls.requirementText: Critical mission functions and cybersecurity risk management are considered to establish post-incident operational norms
controls.guidance: Consider mission-critical functions and updated risk posture when establishing new operational norms following an incident.

controls.title: Restored Asset Verification
controls.requirementText: The integrity of restored assets is verified, systems and services are restored, and normal operating status is confirmed
controls.guidance: Verify the integrity of all restored assets and confirm that systems and services are functioning normally before returning them to production.

controls.title: Recovery Closure
controls.requirementText: The end of incident recovery is declared based on criteria, and incident-related documentation is completed
controls.guidance: Formally declare the end of incident recovery when predefined criteria are met and ensure all incident documentation is completed and archived.

controls.title: Recovery Progress Communication
controls.requirementText: Recovery activities and progress in restoring operational capabilities are communicated to designated internal and external stakeholders
controls.guidance: Provide regular updates on recovery progress to designated stakeholders including estimated timelines for full restoration of services.

controls.title: Public Recovery Updates
controls.requirementText: Public updates on incident recovery are shared using approved methods and messaging
controls.guidance: Communicate public updates about incident recovery using approved channels and pre-reviewed messaging to maintain stakeholder confidence.
